---
name: performance-review
description: Use this skill when writing performance achievement documents, self-assessments, peer feedback, PA documentation, or any career review writing. Trigger on keywords: performance review, PA, self-assessment, peer feedback, performance achievement, review cycle, annual review, accomplishments, sprint contribution.
---

# Performance Review Writing

## Core Principles
- Write only what you actually did — no inflation, no filler phrases
- Use "Assisted in..." when not the primary contributor
- Quantify where possible: coverage %, stories completed, time reduced
- Never use: "demonstrating strong commitment to...", "showcasing my passion for..."
- For peer feedback with limited collaboration: keep general, honest, safe

---

## Self-Assessment Format

### Accomplishment Writing Pattern
```
WEAK:  "Worked on the BTT module"
STRONG: "Built Query Clarity Assessment Agent (US-2111704), 
         achieving 95%+ unit and integration test coverage"

WEAK:  "Helped with UI work"
STRONG: "Assisted in BTT UI/UX enhancements (US-2068094), 
         contributing to LaunchDarkly feature flag implementation"
```

### Personal Reflection (One Flowing Paragraph)
- Write in first person, conversational, honest
- Cover: what you learned, what challenged you, what you want next
- Avoid: bullet points, headers, passive voice, corporate filler

---

## Peer Feedback Templates

**Strength:**
```
[Name] consistently [specific behavior] which [impact].
For example, [concrete but general example].
```

**Growth Area (constructive):**
```
One area where [Name] could develop is [skill/behavior].
I believe strengthening this would [positive outcome].
```

### Limited Collaboration Rule
- Keep it general: work ethic, communication style, team presence
- Do NOT mention specific story IDs
- Only state what you can directly verify

---

## Asking for Feedback
```
Hi [Name], completing my performance review and would value your input.

Two questions if you have a moment:
1. One thing I did well you'd want me to keep doing
2. One area where I could improve

Even a few sentences helps. Thanks!
```

---

## Pre-Submission Checklist
- [ ] Achievements are quantified where possible
- [ ] "Assisted in" used for non-primary contributions
- [ ] No filler phrases remain
- [ ] Personal reflection is prose, not bullets
- [ ] Required fields (EID, dates) are filled in
- [ ] Nothing is overstated
